raw_input:
[4.0/5] works well for home use This electric drill comes with about 11 different drill bits and sanding pieces for the grinders. This also comes with a brush to remove the powder you get from the drill. You also get spreaders for your hands. It also has dust covers for the drill and an emery board. This is strong enough to also use on your pets to grind down their nails. [3.0/5] I'm not sure this is worth the price asked... I've always wanted to get into the art of doing my own acrylics so when I saw this drill set, I knew I had to review it. Firstly, it comes with numerous accessories- a fluffy brush for removing dust from the nail and work area, a set of to separaters for painting, a nail board, and the drill bits themselves. The entire set is nicely packaged and comes nestled in a velvety foam materials. The bits are held in their own clear container within the box. The set comes with a detachable adapter that plugs into the end of the drill and then into the wall outlet. The drill itself is small enough to handle pretty easily, although I can see where it would be difficult for someone with smaller hands than myself (I have large hands.) Inserting and removing the bits can be a challenge. The mechanism isn't well made so I have to fight with it. The power cord is much too short and I had to use an extension cord to give myself some wiggle room away from the wall outlet. Some of the bits' textures do a better job than the others, while some don't do anything at all. The over all look and feel of the set isn't worth the price, in my opinion. It works a bit too awkwardly to be something I'd use that often. [5.0/5] Lots of accessories Nice nail kit, very thorough on the selection of accessories. Works great. [3.0/5] No instructions I was disappointed that this product did not come with instructions. I am new to doing my own nails. I couldn't even figure out how to change the drill heads (I had to get my husband involved and he had trouble getting the lock to release). I would have given this a higher rating if it had just come with instructions (as in this is how you change the drill heads or this drill head is for when you want to do XXX, etc.). That aside, the kit is nice to have for anyone who maintains their nails. [3.0/5] This is a complete Manicure Kit This electric manicure kit has everything you need to use for artificial nails 💅. Well except for the products. The drill is strong and you get a total of 11 bits with it. Be mindful that it is elecric and the switch is on the cord, not the drill itself. Which makes it a little difficult to use. I feel this drill set is not for beginners. You need to have the knowledge on how to use it. In picture #2 I have displayed the instructions that were included. Just one small page and not informative at all.
This is a nice package if your already comfortable with applying artificial nails.
But not for beginners. [3.0/5] Confusing Nail Kit This professional nail kit includes many high quality tools and items to make your nails look beautiful. Unfortunately, I was unable to use them even once because the "instructions" were no help AT ALL. It's a shame because these look very well made and durable.
I did however get some use out of the included nail file and toe separator. But for an item priced at $40 I would expect a clear instruction manual. [1.0/5] Awful This is so awful, I don't know where to begin. Although there are many attachments to this drill, there are absolutely no directions. The small paper that comes with it only had a couple of specs & cautions. I have no idea what most of the drill bits are for. It took forever to figure out how to get the bit that was in it when I got it out. But once I did, it was simple to change. I was trying it out, when I got to the 4th bit, it no longer turned on. I had high hopes, but instead was highly disappointed. Oh well. [1.0/5] Been looking for help I have not been able to try out this nail file as I cannot get the pin out of the top to put attachments in. So, because I can't use it, I cannot give a review. [4.0/5] Surprisingly good for the price For the price, I'm genuinely surprised with this nail drill. First of all, it doesn't suffer from the two major issues most cheap nail drills face: lack of torque and internal wobbling. A lack of torque is when the rotation mechanism stalls out if you press hard enough against the nail. You'll know a nail drill has internal wobbling because it will almost tickle or feel uncomfortable when you file your nails.

With that being said, here's what I don't like: I prefer much thinner nail drills. Even if you "choke up" your grip onto the thinner part of the drill it's still thick enough to make my hands tired if I'm using it for longer than a few minutes. I can't imagine this being comfortable to use all day in a nail salon. Next, the instructions. It took me way too long to figure out how to change the drill bit, partly due to the vague instructions and partly because I had to use so much force I genuinely thought I was going to break it. The twist mechanism is misleading. You turn it and it stops so you think you're done turning it but in actuality you have to keep turning it until you hear a click.
It also would be nice for the manufacturer to explain the differences between all the drill bits and what the intended use case is for each one.

FOR THOSE OF YOU THAT CAN'T CHANGE THE DRILL BIT:

Turn the top portion of the drill toward the side labeled "R" until you hear a click (this is important) use pliers and pull out the drill bit. You'll need to use more force than you think.
Once you've removed the drill bit, stop turning the top portion of the drill toward "R". The mechanism will go back to the center by itself. Pick your desired drill bit and put it where the old drill bit was. Turn the top portion of the drill towards the labeled "S" until you hear a click to secure the drill bit (The drill bit WILL fall out if you don't twist it towards "S" until you hear a click). [4.0/5] Fair quality nail drill for small duties. The product came as described by the seller and delivered on time in good condition. The hard seems to be of good quality; however it's a little loud during operation, the vibration is medium to heavy and I would not recommend using on a virgin nail bed because the lowest speed is a bit High. Great for a full in and very quick small jobs. ( nail tech) [2.0/5] Good for what it is. It does what it’s suppose to do. It’s a decently. made alternative to getting your nails done at the salon. The grinder feels stable and the other products feel well built. It’s good for what it is but is more of a beginning set. [4.0/5] Good product for home use Great for the amateur at home to start with. Love that it has a box to store between use of the product. Appreciate the adjustable speed also. [5.0/5] Top quality. This thing gets the job done and works like a professional tool should. You could burn up dozens of cheap discount nail rotary nail files in less time than you could this one quality rotary nail fail. File, shape and buff like a salon. You won't be disapointed with this one. [4.0/5] Everything you need to do your nails I got this kit as a gift for my daughter. This kit comes with everything you need to do your nails [except polish]. The pictures show what is included in the kit.[one on left]. The other pictures show the drill and the accessories that are included in the box. The drill seems to be well made and sturdy. [5.0/5] Quality, shapes nails, and stylist Pink Great quality and worth every cent. If you want a nail salon in your house than this is perfect and most shops charge you more to take off your nails too. This does it all for you. You can also shape the nails with this product which is nice. [5.0/5] Good value for money I just received my drill ans and i am very satisfied so far will give an update in a years time cause that how long my drills usually last for I'm a professional beauty therapist so this will be used every day at least 6 times per day [4.0/5] Inexpensive Nail Kit That's Versatile and Fun to Use This is a relatively inexpensive nail kit that is versatile and fun to use.

I've never had anything quite like this, and I would have appreciated more thorough instructions on the correct use of all the attachments. As it is, you have to experiment and figure out a lot on your own.

Still, this kit could be used in a salon or for personal use at home. You can manicure and pedicure as well as shape, polish, and remove rough skin, including calluses.

It reminds me a bit of a dremel, so you could probably get creative and use it for hobbies and crafts. I like the fact that it can be charged via a USB cable, which makes it portable. [1.0/5] Still waiting for a full refund!!! First use it was fine! Second time it was stubborn and would stop and go if I gave it a turn, run for a while and stop [4.0/5] Corded electric nail drill and attachments Decent selection of bits for home use on nails. Came with a few extras that are nice if you are just starting to do your own finger or toe nails. This is a plug in the wall electric tool, remember to keep it away from water...and children it is not a toy even though this is in cute pinks and would really be attractive to most kids. Has adjustable speed and seemed to do ok for me, if anything changes I will come change my review. [5.0/5] Strong little device. I think this might be good for diabetics too. It is a great little grinder. And runs by USB power so its very low wattage.
My mother is diabetic and has to be very careful about the shape of her toenails. Its very important to avoid infection. Regular clippers are hard to position correctly sometimes. And there is a risk of 'over cutting' the nail and causing issues that way.
With these you are shaping the nail slowly so you can control it a whole lot better. Less chances of clipper accidents.
**I'm not a doctor nor do I play one on TV. Its just my opinion that these are a good choice for diabetics.
I was given these as a vine promotion for the review. But that aside I like it and see it as a durable useful tool. Mostly because I end up being the one that has to take care of the toenail business And these make it easier to take care of.

response:
Electric nail drill kit. Come with 11 bits, sanding pieces, brush, toe separators, emery board, dust covers. Nicely packaged in velvet foam. Also work on pet nails.

Many reviewers complain about instructions. Instructions bad or missing. Hard to figure out bit changes. One reviewer explain trick: turn top toward R until click, pull bit out with pliers, insert new bit, turn toward S until click.

Drill decent quality for price. Good torque, no wobble. But thick handle make hand tired. Cord short, need extension. Switch on cord not drill, awkward. Loud, heavy vibration. Not good for beginners or virgin nail beds. Some bits work better than others.

Good for home use, amateur nail work, pedicure, callus removal. Work on pet nails too. Adjustable speed nice. USB version portable. One professional beauty therapist use daily, satisfied so far.

Bad reviews mention drill stop working after short use, one person never get pin out at all.

Overall decent kit for home use, not for beginners, need better instructions bad.
